archimate:BusinessObject.name: Permission Policy
archimate:BusinessObject.documentation:
Definition: A permission policy can be defined as a set of rules, guidelines, and controls that govern the authorization and access rights granted to individuals or roles within a system or organization. It outlines the specific permissions and privileges assigned to different entities to ensure appropriate access control and maintain the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of resources or information.
Source: ENISA
Source reference: https://resilience.enisa.europa.eu/article-13/guideline-for-minimum-security-measures/Article_13a_ENISA_Technical_Guideline_On_Security_Measures_v2_0.pdf
LOST view: SV-Governance Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/PermissionPolicyBusinessObject
ABB name: egovera:PermissionPolicyBusinessObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/PermissionPolicyBusinessObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataPolicy

properties.value: A permission policy can be defined as a set of rules, guidelines, and controls that govern the authorization and access rights granted to individuals or roles within a system or organization. It outlines the specific permissions and privileges assigned to different entities to ensure appropriate access control and maintain the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of resources or information.
